data.title: Cathedra Bitcoin Signs Major Hosting Deal Ahead of Sphere 3D Tie-Up
data.description:

Cathedra Bitcoin Inc ( (TSE:CBIT) ) just unveiled an update.

Cathedra Bitcoin Inc., a North American power and digital infrastructure operator serving the bitcoin mining sector, runs four data centers in Tennessee and Kentucky with 45 megawatts of capacity and operates its own high-performance mining fleet. The Vancouver-based company’s shares trade on the TSX Venture Exchange and the OTC market under the symbols CBIT and CBTTF, respectively.

Cathedra has signed a new hosting agreement that will use 80% of the 15-megawatt capacity at its Shire site in Kentucky, representing about a quarter of its total hosting capacity and reinforcing its off-peak energy strategy. The deal broadens its customer base, bolsters recurring revenue, and positions the company more strongly as it moves toward a planned business combination with Sphere 3D Corp., aimed at creating a larger, high-density computing power infrastructure player in North America.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:CBIT) stock is a Sell with a C$0.26 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Cathedra Bitcoin Inc stock, see the TSE:CBIT Stock Forecast page.

Technicals remain bearish (below key moving averages with negative MACD), with oversold readings providing only modest offset. data.title: Strategic Resources Gains Frankfurt Listing to Tap European Demand for Critical Minerals
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Strategic Resources ( (TSE:SR) ).

Strategic Resources Inc., a developer of high-purity iron, vanadium and titanium projects in Canada and Finland, has secured a listing for its common shares on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange under the ticker “UI8N,” while maintaining its primary listing on the TSX Venture Exchange in Canada. Trading in Frankfurt will be conducted in euros, and the company’s regulatory filings will continue to be governed by Canadian securities laws.

The Frankfurt listing responds to growing European investor interest in critical minerals and is intended to broaden Strategic’s access to European capital, improve share liquidity and raise its profile among institutional and retail investors across the EU. Management sees Europe’s leadership in the energy transition as a key demand driver and expects the new listing to support advancement of its flagship BlackRock Project in Quebec and the Mustavaara mine in Finland.

More about Strategic Resources

Strategic Resources Inc. is a Montreal-based critical mineral development company focused on high-purity iron, vanadium and titanium, metals viewed as essential to decarbonizing the global economy. Its key assets include the construction-ready BlackRock Project in Quebec, which plans a 4‑million‑tonne‑per‑year high‑purity iron ore pellet plant at Port Saguenay, and the previously operated Mustavaara mine in Finland.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

data.title: Uranium Energy Begins Production at Burke Hollow Uranium Mine
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Uranium Energy ( (UEC) ).

On April 8, 2026, Uranium Energy Corp announced it had received approval from the Texas Commission on Environmental Quality and begun production at its Burke Hollow project in South Texas, described as the world’s newest in-situ recovery uranium mine. The site is also the first new U.S. ISR uranium operation in more than a decade and will feed output to UEC’s Hobson Central Processing Plant, which is licensed for up to 4 million pounds of uranium per year.

The startup of Burke Hollow, the largest ISR uranium discovery in the U.S. in the past decade, marks a key scale-up of UEC’s domestic production base and gives the company two operating ISR hub-and-spoke platforms alongside Christensen Ranch in Wyoming. With only about half of Burke Hollow’s 20,000-acre property explored and additional wellfields planned, the move strengthens UEC’s role in the U.S. nuclear fuel supply chain and aligns with federal and Texas support for expanding domestic uranium production and energy security.

The most recent analyst rating on (UEC) stock is a Hold with a $13.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Uranium Energy stock, see the UEC Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on UEC Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, UEC is a Neutral.

Overall score reflects a strong strategic/operational outlook and liquidity position from the latest earnings call, partially offset by weak TTM profitability and cash burn. Technical signals remain cautious and valuation is constrained by ongoing losses.

To see Spark’s full report on UEC stock, click here.

More about Uranium Energy

Uranium Energy Corp is a U.S.-based uranium producer focused on in-situ recovery (ISR) mining, controlling the largest uranium resource base and licensed production capacity in the country. The company operates hub-and-spoke ISR platforms in Wyoming and South Texas and holds extensive exploration and development assets in Canada’s Athabasca Basin, while pursuing U.S. refining and conversion capabilities to support the domestic nuclear fuel supply chain.

UEC follows a 100% unhedged uranium strategy, seeking full exposure to uranium market pricing and demand fundamentals. Its management team brings decades of experience across uranium exploration, development, production and fuel cycle infrastructure, positioning the company as a key player in North America’s nuclear fuel ecosystem.

Average Trading Volume: 10,691,485

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $6.43B

data.title: MacroGenics Resumes LINNET Trial After FDA Hold Lifted
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from MacroGenics ( (MGNX) ).

On April 8, 2026, MacroGenics, Inc. announced that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration removed the partial clinical hold on its Phase 2 LINNET study of lorigerlimab in gynecologic cancers, allowing the company to resume enrollment of new participants. During the hold, previously enrolled patients continued treatment, and going forward the trial will proceed under a revised protocol that adds risk-mitigation measures for potential hematologic and cardiac toxicities.

The LINNET study is testing single-agent lorigerlimab, a bispecific DART molecule targeting PD-1 and CTLA-4, in up to about 60 patients with platinum-resistant ovarian cancer or clear cell gynecologic cancer who have received at least one prior therapy. MacroGenics reported that 41 participants have already been dosed in LINNET and more than 300 patients have received lorigerlimab across Phase 1 and Phase 2 trials, underscoring the program’s clinical maturity and the importance of the FDA’s decision for the company’s oncology pipeline and stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (MGNX) stock is a Hold with a $3.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on MacroGenics stock, see the MGNX Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on MGNX Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, MGNX is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by weak financial performance (ongoing losses and significant cash burn) and a negative regulatory event (partial clinical hold tied to serious safety issues). Technicals are supportive via strong trend and positive MACD, but extremely overbought readings increase downside risk. Valuation is constrained by a negative P/E and no dividend yield data.

To see Spark’s full report on MGNX stock, click here.

More about MacroGenics

MacroGenics, Inc. is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing innovative monoclonal antibody-based therapeutics for the treatment of cancer. The Rockville, Md.-based company builds its pipeline from proprietary next-generation antibody platforms, leveraging its protein engineering expertise to advance candidates and secure strategic collaborations with global pharmaceutical and biotechnology partners.

The company’s technology suite underpins programs such as its bispecific DART molecules, designed to engage multiple targets in oncology. These platforms are intended to be broadly applicable across therapeutic domains, positioning MacroGenics as a specialist in complex antibody engineering with a focus on immuno-oncology and partnership-driven development.

Average Trading Volume: 1,090,324

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: $212.9M

Brookdale Senior Living ( (BKD) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On April 8, 2026, Brookdale Senior Living Inc. reported that its consolidated weighted average occupancy for the first quarter of 2026 rose 280 basis points year over year to 82.1 percent, despite a seasonal sequential decline of 40 basis points from the prior quarter. The company noted that this decline was smaller than the historical average first-quarter drop of about 60 basis points, reflecting resilience amid typical winter and holiday-related pressures.

For March 2026, Brookdale’s consolidated weighted average occupancy increased 250 basis points from a year earlier to 82.0 percent, with month-end occupancy improving sequentially to 83.3 percent. Same-community weighted average occupancy for March reached 82.5 percent, up 130 basis points year over year, underscoring continued demand momentum ahead of what the company described as its historically strongest occupancy growth period from roughly May through September.

To see Spark’s full report on BKD stock, click here.

More about Brookdale Senior Living

Brookdale Senior Living Inc. is the nation’s largest operator of senior living communities, with 568 communities across 41 U.S. states serving about 51,000 residents as of March 31, 2026. The company provides independent living, assisted living, memory care, and continuing care retirement communities, leveraging healthcare, hospitality, and real estate expertise to serve aging adults.

data.title: Air Lease Taken Private Following Completion of Merger
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Air Lease ( (AL) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On April 8, 2026, Air Lease Corporation completed a merger in which each share of its Class A common stock was converted into the right to receive $65 in cash, valuing the equity at about $7.4 billion and the overall transaction, including assumed or refinanced debt, at roughly $28.2 billion. The company’s preferred stock remained outstanding with unchanged rights, employee equity awards were cashed out or converted into cash-based awards subject to time vesting, and holders of common stock ceased to have any rights other than to receive the merger payment.

To finance the deal, the surviving entity assumed a $1 billion term loan and obtained access to a $3.5 billion revolving credit facility, while a March 24, 2026 senior notes offering raised $4 billion across maturities from 2028 to 2036 that was placed in escrow until closing. Following completion, the note proceeds were released, became obligations of the company, and were used to repay existing indebtedness and for general corporate purposes, reinforcing the group’s capital structure under its new ownership.

In connection with the merger closing on April 8, 2026, SMBC AC acquired Air Lease’s entire undelivered aircraft orderbook for consideration equal to pre-delivery payments plus a premium, becoming the sole holder of rights to those future deliveries. SMBC AC also entered into a servicing agreement to act as exclusive servicer for the group’s aircraft leased to non-U.S. airlines, handling marketing, trading, technical asset management and risk management, while aircraft leased to U.S. airlines continue to be serviced internally.

Also on April 8, 2026, the company notified the New York Stock Exchange that trading in its Class A common stock and certain medium-term notes would be suspended and withdrawn from listing, with plans to deregister the common stock and suspend related reporting obligations once regulatory delisting is effective. The move effectively takes Air Lease private, removing public equity investors from future participation in its earnings and growth trajectory under its new corporate structure.

At the effective time of the merger, the entire prior board was removed and senior executives including CEO John L. Plueger and CFO Gregory B. Willis departed, becoming entitled to contractual severance packages comprising cash, bonuses and equity vesting. Immediately afterward, a new leadership team was installed, with Sumitomo executive Noriyuki Hiruta appointed chief executive officer and president under a secondment arrangement, and long-time company executive Sabrina Lemmens elevated to chief financial officer under a new compensation and severance framework.

Corporate governance was reshaped as the company adopted restated articles of incorporation and bylaws to govern the surviving corporation post-merger, aligning its charter with the requirements of its new parent ownership. Separately, a stockholder class action filed in November 2025 challenging proxy disclosures was voluntarily dismissed in December 2025 after Air Lease made supplemental disclosures and agreed to pay $450,000 in plaintiff’s counsel fees, without admitting liability, closing a key legal challenge to the transaction.

The most recent analyst rating on (AL) stock is a Hold with a $71.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Air Lease stock, see the AL Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on AL Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, AL is a Neutral.

The score is driven by mixed fundamentals: strong reported profitability and steady operating cash generation are offset by structurally negative free cash flow, leverage risk, and 2025 reporting inconsistencies. Valuation is supportive with a low P/E, and the pending $65 cash-out merger/delisting is a significant positive event. Technicals are moderately positive with an uptrend and stable momentum.

To see Spark’s full report on AL stock, click here.

More about Air Lease

Air Lease Corporation operates in the aircraft leasing industry, providing commercial jet leasing and related asset management services to airlines worldwide. The company focuses on acquiring new aircraft from major manufacturers and placing them on long-term leases, with a portfolio diversified across U.S. and non-U.S. carriers and a sizable orderbook of undelivered aircraft that underpins its growth strategy.

Air Lease’s model relies heavily on access to global capital markets and bank financing to fund fleet expansion, using a mix of secured and unsecured debt, term loans, and revolving credit facilities. Its revenue and earnings are driven by lease rentals, aircraft trading gains, and the effective management of residual values and airline credit risk across economic cycles.

Average Trading Volume: 2,070,600

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $7.28B

For a thorough assessment of AL stock, go to TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.
